articles.title: Teens are sleeping less than ever and screens aren’t primarily to blame - NPR
articles.description: Nearly a quarter of teens sleep 5 hours or less per night and the majority sleep less than 8 hours. The problem is pervasive and technology doesn't seem to be the main culprit, according to a new report.

articles.description: Drugs best known for treating diabetes and obesity may also dampen the powerful cravings that drive addiction.
